name: ABI Package Examples
description: Create runnable ABI (Application Binary Interface) examples for the @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/abi subpackage. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>/index.ts`. The ABI package provides type encoding/decoding, method management, and ARC-56 support for Algorand smart contracts.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared utilities and constants for all ABI examples so that examples have consistent setup and helper functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/shared/constants.ts with any common constants needed for ABI examples
Create examples/abi/shared/utils.ts with: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatBytes(), formatHex()
Create examples/abi/package.json referencing file:../../dist for @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils
Create examples/abi/tsconfig.json with ESM module settings
Create examples/abi/README.md documenting all ABI examples
npm install runs without errors in examples/abi directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: ABI Type Parsing Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to parse ABI type strings into type objects using ABIType.from().
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/01-type-parsing/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIType.from() parsing various type strings
Parse primitive types: uint8, uint64, uint256, bool, byte, address, string
Parse array types: uint64[], byte[32], address[5]
Parse tuple types: (uint64,address), (bool,string,uint256)
Show type properties: toString(), bitSize (for uint), staticLength (for static arrays)
Demonstrate type category detection using instanceof checks
Run the example with `npx tsx 01-type-parsing/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Primitive Types Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode primitive ABI types (uint, bool, byte).
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/02-primitive-types/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIUintType for various bit sizes: 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512
Show encoding BigInt values for large uint types (uint256, uint512)
Demonstrate ABIBoolType encoding true/false to single byte
Demonstrate ABIByteType encoding single byte values
Show encode() and decode() methods for each type
Display encoded bytes in hex format for clarity
Verify round-trip: decode(encode(value)) equals original value
Run the example with `npx tsx 02-primitive-types/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Address Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode Algorand addresses using ABIAddressType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/03-address-type/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIAddressType encoding address strings to 32 bytes
Show encoding from base32 address string format
Show encoding from raw 32-byte public key (Uint8Array)
Decode bytes back to address string
Verify address encoding is exactly 32 bytes (no length prefix)
Show relationship between Algorand address and public key bytes
Run the example with `npx tsx 03-address-type/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: String Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode dynamic strings using ABIStringType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/04-string-type/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIStringType encoding strings with length prefix
Show the 2-byte length prefix followed by UTF-8 encoded content
Encode various strings: empty string, ASCII, Unicode characters
Show how dynamic types include their length in the encoding
Decode strings back from bytes
Display byte breakdown: length prefix vs content bytes
Run the example with `npx tsx 04-string-type/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Static Array Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode fixed-length arrays using ABIArrayStaticType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/05-static-array/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIArrayStaticType with various element types
Show byte[32] encoding (fixed 32 bytes, common for hashes)
Show uint64[3] encoding (fixed array of integers)
Show address[2] encoding (fixed array of addresses)
Demonstrate that static arrays have no length prefix
Show how element types are encoded consecutively
Verify encoded length matches expected: elementSize * arrayLength
Run the example with `npx tsx 05-static-array/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Dynamic Array Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode variable-length arrays using ABIArrayDynamicType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/06-dynamic-array/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIArrayDynamicType with various element types
Show uint64[] encoding with 2-byte length prefix
Show string[] encoding demonstrating head/tail encoding for nested dynamic types
Encode arrays of different lengths to show dynamic sizing
Show the head/tail encoding pattern for arrays containing dynamic elements
Display byte layout: length prefix, head section, tail section
Run the example with `npx tsx 06-dynamic-array/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Tuple Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode tuples with mixed types using ABITupleType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/07-tuple-type/index.ts
Demonstrate ABITupleType with mixed static types (uint64,bool,address)
Demonstrate tuples with dynamic types (uint64,string,bool)
Show head/tail encoding: static values inline, dynamic values referenced
Demonstrate nested tuples: ((uint64,bool),string)
Show how tuple elements are accessed after decoding
Display byte layout for tuples with and without dynamic elements
Run the example with `npx tsx 07-tuple-type/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Struct Type Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode named structs using ABIStructType.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/08-struct-type/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIStructType creation with named fields
Create a struct like: { name: string, age: uint64, active: bool }
Show encoding struct values as objects with named keys
Demonstrate that struct encoding is identical to equivalent tuple encoding
Show struct field names and types via the type object
Decode back to struct value with named fields
Run the example with `npx tsx 08-struct-type/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Struct and Tuple Conversion Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to convert between struct values (named) and tuple values (positional).
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/09-struct-tuple-conversion/index.ts
Demonstrate getTupleValueFromStructValue() function
Demonstrate getStructValueFromTupleValue() function
Show converting a struct { name: 'Alice', age: 30n } to tuple ['Alice', 30n]
Show converting a tuple back to struct with field names
Demonstrate with nested structs and complex types
Verify that struct and tuple encodings produce identical bytes
Run the example with `npx tsx 09-struct-tuple-conversion/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Bool Array Packing Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how bool arrays are packed efficiently (8 bools per byte).
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/10-bool-packing/index.ts
Demonstrate static bool array encoding: bool[8] fits in 1 byte
Show how 8 bools are packed into a single byte as bits
Demonstrate bool[16] encoding into 2 bytes
Show bool arrays that don't fill a complete byte (e.g., bool[5])
Compare encoded size of bool[] vs uint8[] for same count
Decode packed bool arrays back to boolean values
Display bit-level view of packed booleans
Run the example with `npx tsx 10-bool-packing/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: ABI Method Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to parse method signatures and generate method selectors.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/11-abi-method/index.ts
Demonstrate ABIMethod.fromSignature() to parse method signatures
Parse various signatures: 'transfer(address,uint64)uint64', 'hello(string)string'
Show method name, args array, and returns type
Demonstrate getSelector() to generate 4-byte method selector
Show the relationship: first 4 bytes of SHA-512/256 of signature
Parse method with no args: 'get()uint64'
Parse method with no return: 'set(uint64)void'
Parse method with tuple args and returns
Run the example with `npx tsx 11-abi-method/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: AVM Type Encoding Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to encode and decode AVM-specific types (bytes, string, uint64).
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/12-avm-types/index.ts
Demonstrate AVMBytes type for raw byte arrays
Demonstrate AVMString type for AVM stack strings
Demonstrate AVMUint64 type for 64-bit unsigned integers
Show encodeAVMValue() for encoding values to AVM representation
Show decodeAVMValue() for decoding AVM bytes to values
Compare AVM encoding vs ABI encoding for same values
Show when to use AVM types vs ABI types
Run the example with `npx tsx 12-avm-types/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Type Guards Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use type guard functions to check argument and type categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/13-type-guards/index.ts
Demonstrate argTypeIsTransaction() to check for transaction arg types
Demonstrate argTypeIsReference() to check for reference types (account, asset, app)
Demonstrate argTypeIsAbiType() to check for standard ABI types
Demonstrate isAVMType() to check for AVM-specific types
Show how these guards help with method argument handling
Test guards against various type strings: 'txn', 'pay', 'account', 'asset', 'application', 'uint64'
Show proper TypeScript usage with type narrowing
Run the example with `npx tsx 13-type-guards/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Complex Nested Types Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to work with deeply nested ABI types combining arrays, tuples, and structs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/14-complex-nested/index.ts
Demonstrate array of tuples: (uint64,string)[]
Demonstrate tuple containing arrays: (uint64[],string[])
Demonstrate nested structs with arrays
Show deeply nested type: ((uint64,bool)[],string,(address,uint256))[]
Encode and decode complex nested values
Display byte layout showing head/tail structure
Verify round-trip encoding/decoding preserves all nested values
Run the example with `npx tsx 14-complex-nested/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all ABI examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 14)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
Add npm script 'verify-all' to package.json
All 14 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh

userStories.title: ARC-56 Storage Helpers Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use ARC-56 storage helpers to read typed state keys and maps from deployed contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/abi/15-arc56-storage/index.ts
Load an ARC-56 contract spec (State.arc56.json from tests/example-contracts/state)
Demonstrate getGlobalABIStorageKeys() to get all global state key definitions
Demonstrate getLocalABIStorageKeys() to get all local state key definitions
Demonstrate getBoxABIStorageKeys() to get all box key definitions
Show ABIStorageKey properties: key, keyType, valueType, desc
Demonstrate getBoxABIStorageMaps() to get box map definitions
Show ABIStorageMap properties: keyType, valueType, desc, prefix
Deploy the State contract to LocalNet and read actual state values
Use the storage key types to decode raw state bytes into typed values
Run the example with `npx tsx 15-arc56-storage/index.ts` and verify it completes successfully
If LocalNet errors occur (e.g., insufficient funds, network issues), run `algokit localnet reset` and retry
